definition: EM reserve adequacy has bifurcated sharply between commodity exporters (15-25 months import cover) and importers with managed exchange rates (2-4 months). Guidotti-Greenspan ratio below 1.0 for 12-15 EM economies versus 8-10 in 2019, indicating deteriorating coverage.
evidence: Saudi Arabia ~$420B (18+ months), UAE ~$180B vs. Egypt ~$35B (~3.5 months), Pakistan ~$9B (~2 months), Ethiopia ~$1.5B (~1 month)
Guidotti-Greenspan ratio below 1.0 for 12-15 EM economies vs. 8-10 in 2019
open_questions: Which of the 12-15 economies below Guidotti-Greenspan 1.0 face nearest-term stress?
How quickly can reserve drawdown accelerate under capital flight?
Does the bifurcation amplify contagion risk between commodity-linked and importing EMs?
